description_long: This object consists of three thick, rectangular sandwich pieces with a visible breaded cutlet filling, likely pork katsu, layered between soft, white sandwich bread. The sandwiches are partially wrapped in a white paper wrapper, which is folded back to reveal the contents. Next to the sandwiches is a golden-brown cardboard box with Japanese text and branding, suggesting a takeout or convenience food item. The bread appears fluffy and slightly compressed, while the cutlet inside is golden and crispy, with a dark sauce visible along the cut edge. The sandwiches and box rest on a round, light-colored surface, possibly a tray or plate. The overall presentation is neat and typical of Japanese convenience store or takeout food packaging.
description: Three thick katsu sandwiches in white bread are partially wrapped in paper, accompanied by a branded golden cardboard box, all set on a round tray.
description_view_0: Three sandwich pieces are clearly visible with the cutlet filling exposed, and the branded box is positioned behind them.
description_view_1: The sandwiches are seen from the side, showing the thickness of the bread and filling, with the box more prominently displayed in the foreground.
description_view_2: The branded box is in the foreground, with the sandwiches receding into the background, emphasizing the packaging details.
description_view_3: The sandwiches are viewed from the opposite side, with the box behind them and the paper wrapper more visible.
